hawkish_phrases: inflation had been persistently below levels consistent with the Governing Council's inflation aim
inflation expectations remained close to their historical lows
stand ready to adjust all of its instruments, as appropriate
recent appreciation of the euro exchange rate had had a material impact on the inflation outlook
dovish_phrases: inflation pressures were expected to remain subdued on account of weak demand, lower wage pressures and the appreciation of the euro exchange rate
inflation was projected to be 1.3% in 2022, thereby remaining below the projected pre-crisis path
headline inflation was likely to remain negative over the coming months
ample monetary policy stimulus would remain necessary to support the economic recovery
the balance of risks to the euro area growth outlook was seen to remain on the downside
summary: The Governing Council held all policy rates unchanged (deposit facility at −0.50%) and maintained the PEPP envelope at €1,350 billion with a clear dovish bias, emphasizing subdued inflation pressures, downside growth risks, concern over euro appreciation dampening the inflation outlook, and readiness to adjust all instruments as appropriate.
